title: helmia coriacea
intro: Helmia coriacea is a plant species, also known as the paperbark fig, native to the tropical regions of the Pacific Islands and Australia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Helmia coriacea include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections. The herbal preparations of Helmia coriacea often involve making a decoction or infusion from its bark or leaves, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
